name: Sur Le Gril Chopped Liver
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the tough outer membranes from the liver using a sharp knife.
2. In a heavy skillet, heat the oil and add the chopped onions. Cook over medium-high heat until translucent and golden brown. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
3. Add butter to the skillet and melt it. Add the liver pieces and fry over medium heat for 3 minutes on each side. Alternatively, brush the liver with butter and broil.
4. To test for doneness, cut a piece from the liver slice. If still pink, continue cooking for 30-60 seconds. Overcooking causes the liver to be dry and tough.
5. For finely mashed egg that disappears in the liver mixture, force the egg through a wire strainer. Separate the hard-cooked whites from yolks and sieve them separately.
6. Place the cooked liver, cooked onions, sieved eggs, salt, and pepper into a medium mixing bowl. Combine well.
7. Taste and adjust seasoning as desired. If desired, add a few drops of commercial gravy brown liquid to enhance color.
8. Refrigerate for 24 hours before serving. Serve with slices of tomato, cucumber, and dark rye bread. Serves 4.
